steps.title: Identify what comes after “obligations”
steps.content: The sentence moves from introducing NATO's collective defense principle to explaining what the obligations entail. The phrase “while the organization's many members...are all bound to defend Spain, the reverse is also true” elaborates on how these obligations work in practice: members defend Spain, and Spain must defend them in return.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content: A colon correctly introduces an elaboration or explanation of an independent clause. Choice D's colon signals that what follows elaborates on “obligations”—it shows the mutual nature of the defense commitment. Choices A and B fragment this into separate statements, weakening the explanatory connection between the obligation and its meaning. Choice C uses a comma, which is insufficient for a substantial elaboration. This matches choice D — D is correct.
